title: Towerless vertical-axis wind turbine
abstract: The present disclosure is directed to towerless vertical-axis wind turbines with pre- tensioned rotors. The present disclosure is further directed to a vertical-axis wind turbine (VAWT) with supported blade ends (e.g., Darrieus type) which replaces the center tower with tensioned supports, such as tensioned guy wires, and blade pre-load.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A vertical-axis wind turbine, comprising: two or more blades having an upper end, a lower end and a height; and one or more flexible connectors attached to the two or more blade, each flexible connector of the one or more flexible connections having a length; wherein the vertical-axis wind turbine does not include a center axis rigid support attached to the two or more blades upper ends. 2. The vertical-axis wind turbine of claim 1, further comprising: one or more adjustment units that increase and decrease the length of the one or more flexible connectors that increases or decreases the height of the two or more blades; and a control unit that sends a command to the adjustment unit to increase and decrease the length of the one or more flexible commands. 3. The vertical-axis wind turbine of claim 2, wherein the control unit includes a processor that receives commands from one or more sensors. 4. The vertical-axis wind turbine of claim 2, wherein the one or more sensors provide sensed data of one or more parameters selected from the group consisting of wind speed, wave height, platform acceleration and rotor stress states to the control unit. 5. The vertical-axis wind turbine of claim 1, further comprising: a base connector attached to the lower end of the two or more connectors, wherein the base connector is attached to a base. 6. The vertical-axis wind turbine of claim 4, wherein the base is a sea platform. 7. The vertical-axis wind turbine of claim 1, further comprising: a top connector attached to the upper end of the two or more blades. 8. The vertical-axis wind turbine of claim 6, wherein the upper end of the two or more blades are attached to the top connector by a pin or hinge. 9. The vertical-axis wind turbine of claim 2, wherein at least one of the one or more adjustment unit comprises a motor and a spool for winding thereupon a portion of the length of the one or more flexible connectors. 10. The vertical-axis wind turbine of claim 1 , wherein the one or more flexible connectors include a central flexible connector aligned with the vertical axis of the vertical-axis wind turbine. 11. The vertical-axis wind turbine of claim 2, wherein the one or more adjustment units is one adjustment unit. 12. The vertical-axis wind turbine of claim 1, wherein the one or more flexible connectors are steel cables. 13. A method of controlling a vertical-axis wind turbine, comprising: lengthening and shortening one or more flexible connectors attached to two or more blades of the vertical-axis wind turbine to adjust tension upon the two or more blades by increasing and decreasing the axial length of the two or more blades. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the one or more flexible connectors is lengthened or shortened in response to one or more sensed conditions. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the one or more sensed conditions is selected from the group consisting of wind speed, wave height, platform acceleration and rotor stress states. 16. The method of 13, wherein the one or more flexible connectors includes a central flexible connector aligned with the vertical axis of the vertical-axis wind turbine. 17. The method of claim 13, wherein the two or more blades are lengthened and shortened by a single length adjustment unit. 18. The method of supporting two or more blades of a vertical-axis wind turbine comprising: attaching one or more flexible connectors to upper portions of the two or more blades to compress the blades into an operational configuration; wherein the two or more blades are not connected to a rigid, vertical support. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the one or more flexible connectors is a metal or composite cable. 20. The method of claim 18, wherein a bottom portion of the two or more blades is connected to a sea platform.
